setupV2.hook.welcome.title: Welcome to GrowBase
setupV2.hook.welcome.quote: Do not save what is left after spending, but spend what is left after saving.
setupV2.hook.welcome.body: The next screen is the financial picture of a sample family — the Minnies — built from their last two months of data. Your family's picture will take shape the same way.
setupV2.hook.welcome.aspect.flow: Money coming in and going out each month
setupV2.hook.welcome.aspect.savings: Savings and the funds being built
setupV2.tour.income.title: Income this month
setupV2.tour.income.body: Total money coming in — the starting point of the whole picture. Every expense and every bit of savings flows from here.
setupV2.tour.expense.title: Spending this month
setupV2.tour.expense.body: Total money spent and how it compares to last month. Keeping spending below income is what moves the picture upward.
setupV2.tour.savings.title: Money set aside
setupV2.tour.savings.body: What the family actively keeps and moves into funds each month — the gap between income and spending turned into savings.
setupV2.tour.health.title: Financial health
setupV2.tour.health.body: The share of income kept — a quick read on the household's overall financial health.
setupV2.tour.incomeExpense.title: Income vs spending
setupV2.tour.incomeExpense.body: This month's money in against money out, next to last month — a quick look at whether cash flow is improving.
setupV2.tour.spending.title: Spending breakdown
setupV2.tour.spending.body: Spending split by behavior: essential, discretionary, and cuttable. Shows where the money really goes.
setupV2.tour.topExpenses.title: Biggest spending groups
setupV2.tour.topExpenses.body: The groups that ate the most this month — the first place to look when you want to save.
setupV2.tour.weekday.title: Spending by weekday
setupV2.tour.weekday.body: Spending habits spread across the days of the week — spot the day the wallet opens widest.
setupV2.tour.budget.title: Budget by group
setupV2.tour.budget.body: Actual spending against the budget set for each group — the fuller the bar, the closer to the limit.
setupV2.tour.funds.title: Funds being built
setupV2.tour.funds.body: The emergency fund as a safety base, and goal funds for the family's longer-term dreams.
setupV2.tour.recentTx.title: Recent transactions
setupV2.tour.recentTx.body: A log of each amount in and out — the foundation of the whole picture. The more consistently logged, the truer the numbers.
setupV2.tour.finale.title: Now for your family's picture
setupV2.tour.finale.body: That was the Minnies' picture. To build your own, we start with the foundation stone: your fixed monthly income.
setupV2.goal.title: What do you want for your family?
setupV2.goal.emergency.name: Emergency fund
setupV2.goal.emergency.blurb: A 3-6 month buffer, built first. When shocks hit (job loss, illness) they won't shatter your money plan.
setupV2.goal.tier2Title: Your foundation is solid. Now pick the goals you want to aim for
setupV2.goal.education.name: Education fund
setupV2.goal.house.name: Buy a home
setupV2.goal.travel.name: Travel
setupV2.goal.custom.name: Custom goal
setupV2.goal.editor.namePlaceholder: e.g. A car for the family
setupV2.goal.editor.invalidHint: Enter a name and target amount to continue
setupV2.goal.suggestNever: Would take over {{max}} months at your current income
setupV2.goal.suggestEscape: Don't worry — you'll find ways to shorten this in the planning step
setupV2.goal.rankSubtitle: Drag to reorder — higher-ranked funds are funded first
setupV2.goal.adviseHint: Done {{delta}} months sooner if you move it up to priority {{rank}}
setupV2.goal.compoundLine: ~{{months}} months left if you contribute via {{channel}}
setupV2.goal.compoundDisclaimer: Rates referenced from {{year}} — for reference only, not a commitment or investment advice
setupV2.goal.splitPrompt: Split into milestones for breathing room?
setupV2.income.title: What is your family's fixed monthly income?
setupV2.income.householdHint: Count the whole household — the money that comes in steadily every month, like salaries and fixed allowances. A rough number is fine.
setupV2.income.excludeHint: Not counting one-off income: surprise bonuses, selling things, gift money, or irregular earnings — you'll log those separately later.
setupV2.income.whyFixed.title: Why start with fixed income?
setupV2.income.whyFixed.body: This baseline sets your emergency fund target, splits money evenly into funds each month, and keeps goals realistic. A steady number means a steady plan — one-off income is logged separately, not mixed in here.
setupV2.income.error: Please enter an income greater than 0 — a rough number works
setupV2.income.emergencyAutoDesc: For reference: 3 × your monthly spending based on the income you entered — every family should have this.
setupV2.tada.pending.goal: Attaching your goal to the plan...
setupV2.tada.capacitySource: = {{percent}}% of income set aside for Savings & Investment, split across your funds
setupV2.tada.threeStage.withGoals: Stage 1: safe {{months}} mo → 2: safe + saving for dreams → 3: all in on dreams.
setupV2.tada.threeStage.emergencyOnly: Stage 1: safety in the first {{months}} months → Stage 2: top up your emergency fund until it's solid.
setupV2.tada.threeStage.noMonthsWithGoal: The plan moves through 3 stages: safety first, then safety while building toward your dreams, then all in on your dreams.
setupV2.tada.threeStage.noMonthsEmergenc: The plan puts building a solid emergency fund first.
setupV2.tada.threeStage.readyWithGoals: Your emergency fund is ready from the start → now go all in on your dreams.
setupV2.tada.threeStage.readyEmergencyOn: Your emergency fund is ready from the start — your foundation is already solid.
setupV2.tada.attribution: Methods referenced: Conscious Spending Plan (Ramit Sethi) · debt fund 70/20/10 (Clason) · emergency fund per CFPB
welcome.title: Your family's financial picture
welcome.subtitle: Here are your household's numbers as of today.
welcome.errorDesc: You can still open the dashboard to see the details.
setupV2.plan.title: Detailed plan
setupV2.plan.subtitle: How the numbers above are built — open it when you want the full picture.
setupV2.plan.budgetLead: Every dong of income is pre-split into four buckets.
setupV2.plan.capacityNote: The Savings & investment bucket ({{amount}}/mo) is what flows into your funds.
setupV2.plan.weightLead: Higher-priority goals get a larger share on a ladder.
setupV2.plan.emergencyFirst: Emergency fund: foundation first — funded ahead by stage.
setupV2.plan.stageLead: Contributions shift focus across each stage.
setupV2.plan.stage1: Stage 1 — send 100% to the emergency fund until it covers 1 month of essentials.
setupV2.plan.stage2: Stage 2 — split 70% emergency / 30% goals until the fund reaches 3 months.
setupV2.plan.stage3: Stage 3 — send 100% to goals by priority weight.
setupV2.plan.channelLead: For distant goals, a yield channel can shorten the journey.
setupV2.tada.fundTimelineNever: Over {{max}} months at the current pace
setupV2.tada.fundNotFound: Couldn't find the fund to adjust — continuing to the dashboard anyway.
setupV2.tada.noAdjustHint: No goal funds to adjust yet — you can add them later on the Funds page.
setupV2.tada.todayRemainingHint: = {{percent}}% of income for flexible spending, split across {{days}} days this month
setupV2.tada.hookCallback: That was just an example. This is your own number.
setupV2.tada.rationale.mentalAccounting: Splitting money into separate funds helps you stay consistent — the 'mental accounting' principle by Richard Thaler (Nobel Prize in Economics, 2017).
setupV2.tada.errorTitle: Something went wrong setting up your account
setupV2.tada.errorDesc: We couldn't finish setting things up — check your connection and try again.
setupV2.tada.alreadyOnboardedDesc: Your account already exists — head to the Dashboard to continue.
insight.firstDay: Today you have {{remainingToday}} to spend comfortably.
insight.underPlanYesterday: Yesterday you spent {{yesterdaySpent}} — {{yesterdayDiff}} under plan. That extra pushed {{goalName}} forward a bit 🎉. Today you have {{remainingToday}} left.
insight.underPlanYesterdayNoGoal: Yesterday you spent {{yesterdaySpent}} — {{yesterdayDiff}} under plan 🎉. Today you have {{remainingToday}} left.
insight.overPlanYesterday: Yesterday you spent {{yesterdaySpent}}, a bit over plan by {{yesterdayDiff}}. No worries — today you have {{remainingToday}} to even it out.
insight.noTransactionsYesterday: No transactions logged yesterday — today you have {{remainingToday}} to spend.
insight.goalAhead: {{goalName}} is ahead of plan — keep this pace and you'll get there {{monthsEarly}} months early 🎉
insight.goalBehind: {{goalName}} is a bit behind plan — an extra {{catchUpAmount}} would catch it up.
insight.goalOnTrack: {{goalName}} is right on track — steady as you go.
celebration.milestoneBody: {{goalName}} has reached {{milestone}}% of the way — the whole family is doing great!
celebration.goalCompletedBody: {{goalName}} made it! You did it together 🎉
setup.invited.title: You are invited
setup.invited.subtitle: You have a pending invitation to join a household
